title: Multiple-choice cloze
description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.

content.text: Urban planners are increasingly concerned about the psychological effects of overcrowded cities. Early studies (0) maintained that population density alone caused stress, but recent research suggests the issue is more nuanced.||Experts argue that stress levels rise when residents cannot (1) control over their environment. Noise pollution, for instance, can (2) concentration and reduce overall wellbeing.||To address this, some cities have begun to (3) green corridors that provide quiet spaces for relaxation. These areas not only improve air quality but also (4) opportunities for social interaction.||However, implementing such initiatives is not straightforward. Budget constraints often (5) progress, and political disagreements can (6) long-term planning. Planners must therefore (7) competing interests and persuade stakeholders to (8) sustainable urban solutions.

example.explanation: “Maintained” is the correct verb for presenting early academic claims.

questions.explanation: “Impair concentration” is the natural academic phrasing.
“Establish green corridors” is idiomatic in urban planning.
